mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and consumer credit indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 2% over a single-series baseline, or forward returns in contractionary regimes are not statistically lower than expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5%, aggressive federal funds rate hikes trigger liquidity crunches rather than cooling demand, causing negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when both high inflation and rate hikes coincide, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding economic activity that boosts corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth rates, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in business investment and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts breach the threshold, or the signal hit rate falls below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls and rising interest rates creates a cost-push inflation spiral that forces monetary over-tightening, crushing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict equity declines when both series rise concurrently, or forward returns in high-wage/high-rate regimes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a rapid rebound in industrial production combined with stable consumer credit fuels a profit-driven equity rally, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not outperform when industrial production and credit expand simultaneously, or the interaction effect reverses sign during stagflationary periods.
